text: fat is 13g. this is the kind of fat profile you actually want from a meat. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
carbs is 0g. the rest is not doing much. in this category, carbs barely matter.
protein is 20.4g. protein quality is a real strength here. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
vitamin b12 at 117% DV, vitamin d at 66% DV stand out most. that is major vitamin support.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

on the plus side. easy to build a strong meal around because it works as a genuine centrepiece instead of needing lots of nutritional excuses. very satisfying as a protein source because that makes it practical in real meals. has unusually strong trust with viewers because people already read it as a serious healthy-food choice, which matches its overall case.

now the downsides. can be expensive because price can make it less practical. quality varies with sourcing because sourcing changes the real-world quality. needs good storage and handling because it is less convenient than shelf-stable options.
S tier. Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
